tweets.title: Pakistan's terror infrastructure never disappears. It simply changes its face. Senior Lashkar-E-Taiba/ PMML leaders, Chaudhry Shafiq ur Rehman Warraich, Yasir Arif Butt, and Inam ur Rehman Kamboh, met former PoJK PM Sardar Tanveer Ilyas to offer condolences after the attack on his convoy that killed his former SSG commando and personal security guard, Muhammad Asif. When the political face of Lashkar e Taiba openly meets a former PoJK PM, Pakistan still expects the world to believe its terror networks have been dismantled.
tweets.content:
Pakistan's terror infrastructure never disappears. It simply changes its face.

Senior Lashkar-E-Taiba/ PMML leaders, Chaudhry Shafiq ur Rehman Warraich, Yasir Arif Butt, and Inam ur Rehman Kamboh, met former PoJK PM Sardar Tanveer Ilyas to offer condolences after the attack on his convoy that killed his former SSG commando and personal security guard, Muhammad Asif.

When the political face of Lashkar e Taiba openly meets a former PoJK PM, Pakistan still expects the world to believe its terror networks have been dismantled.


Video
tweets.author: @OsintTV

tweets.title: RT by @sidhant: “If rivers could speak, perhaps the Jhelum would ask, ‘What was my fault?’” For nearly four decades, Pakistan’s objections under the Indus Waters Treaty kept the Tulbul Navigation Project frozen. Even after 14 rounds of bilateral talks, Pakistan did not withdraw its opposition and the deadlock remained unresolved. Pakistan raised the objections. Kashmir paid the price. Watch my latest analysis on one of Kashmir’s longest-running water disputes
tweets.content:
“If rivers could speak, perhaps the Jhelum would ask, ‘What was my fault?’”

For nearly four decades, Pakistan’s objections under the Indus Waters Treaty kept the Tulbul Navigation Project frozen. Even after 14 rounds of bilateral talks, Pakistan did not withdraw its opposition and the deadlock remained unresolved.
Pakistan raised the objections. Kashmir paid the price.
Watch my latest analysis on one of Kashmir’s longest-running water disputes

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: Pakistanis often reject the “beggar” label, but this discussion tells a different story. Anchor: Pakistan has demanded a $10 billion brokerage fee from the US (for brokering a deal between the US and Iran) Guest 1: “Bohot, bohot acha kiya Pakistan ne unse maang kar.” I hope Pakistan has no intention of returning this money. This is the least the world owes us. If we ask America today, not just $10 billion, we might get a lot more. We should demand money from other countries too. Guest 2: “Americans, every bill is accounted for.” People ask, what are we getting in return for this mediation? If we are putting in so much effort, what are we getting back? A mediation role comes with a price tag now. ..😄 Video Courtesy: Hum news
tweets.content:
Pakistanis often reject the “beggar” label, but this discussion tells a different story.

Anchor: Pakistan has demanded a $10 billion brokerage fee from the US (for brokering a deal between the US and Iran)

Guest 1: “Bohot, bohot acha kiya Pakistan ne unse maang kar.” I hope Pakistan has no intention of returning this money. This is the least the world owes us. If we ask America today, not just $10 billion, we might get a lot more. We should demand money from other countries too.

Guest 2: “Americans, every bill is accounted for.” People ask, what are we getting in return for this mediation? If we are putting in so much effort, what are we getting back?

A mediation role comes with a price tag now. ..😄

Video Courtesy: Hum news


Video
tweets.author: @OsintTV

tweets.title: There must be zero tolerance for terrorism. Nor should there be any doubt that acts of terrorism will have consequences, EAM Jaishankar at Asean Region Forum meet in Manila
tweets.content:
There must be zero tolerance for terrorism. Nor should there be any doubt that acts of terrorism will have consequences, EAM Jaishankar at Asean Region Forum meet in Manila

Dr. S. Jaishankar (@DrSJaishankar)

Spoke at the 33rd ASEAN Regional Forum (ARF) Ministerial Meeting today in Manila.

➡️ Recognised the importance of ARF for discussing political & security issues in the Indo-Pacific, especially in these complex times.

➡️ Welcomed the Manila Plan of Action and highlighted that its five pillars are aligned with India’s own approach to the Indo-Pacific.

➡️ India promotes a collaborative approach to disaster relief, sharing best practices, developing common SOPs and undertaking real-time & table top exercises.

➡️ Shared that India advocates for the Coalition for Disaster Resilient Infrastructure, regularly deploys field hospitals, provides search & rescue, sends relief materials & medical supplies.

➡️ India has undertaken rehabilitation recently ranging from the cyclone in Sri Lanka, the Philippines and Jamaica to earthquakes in Myanmar, Venezuela and Afghanistan.

➡️ There must be zero tolerance for terrorism. Nor should there be any doubt that acts of terrorism will have consequences.

➡️ We should prioritize choking the financial resources that fuel it. India co-chairs the EWG with Malaysia in ADMM+, taking the lead in producing a compendium of best practices.

➡️ On maritime security, shared that the information Fusion Centre in India, the Indo-Pacific Maritime Domain Awareness initiative, deployment of Indian forces from the North Arabian Sea to the Western Pacific, are making a valuable contribution to counter piracy, trafficking and narcotics trade as well as secure maritime commerce.

➡️ Stressed the need for the International community to be on guard against those who undermine non-proliferation and disarmament.

➡️ Advocated a responsible and human-centric approach on AI and deeper cooperation on cybercrimes and other issues.

➡️ Highlighted that attacks on maritime shipping and civilian infrastructure are unacceptable, and commerce through international waterways should remain safe and unimpeded.

➡️ On South China Sea, underlined that we look forward to a substantive, effective and legally binding CoC fully compliant with UNCLOS 1982 that does not prejudice the legitimate rights of all users.

— https://nitter.net/DrSJaishankar/status/2080143965547086218#m
tweets.author: @sidhant

tweets.title: Good morning sir 👏
tweets.content:
Good morning sir 👏

Narendra Modi (@narendramodi)

Nothing is more important than the welfare and future of our youth!

We have decided to set up fast-track courts to ensure swift and stringent punishment for those involved in paper leaks. Have directed the concerned authorities and officials to take all necessary steps in this regard. This continues our series of steps for safeguarding the interests of students.

Those who try to harm the future of our youth will not be spared.

देश के युवाओं का हित और उनका भविष्य हमारी सर्वोच्च प्राथमिकता है।

पेपर लीक के सभी मामलों में दोषियों को जल्द से जल्द कठोर दंड देने के लिए, केस के त्वरित निस्तारण के लिए फास्ट ट्रैक कोर्ट्स का गठन किया जाएगा। इस संबंध में निर्देश दे दिए गए हैं कि संबंधित विभाग आवश्यक कार्रवाई सुनिश्चित करें।

विद्यार्थियों के हितों को सुरक्षित करने के लिए, सरकार द्वारा लिए जा रहे निर्णयों की श्रृंखला में ये एक महत्वपूर्ण कदम है।

युवाओं के भविष्य के साथ खिलवाड़ करने वाले किसी भी व्यक्ति को बख्शा नहीं जाएगा।

tweets.title: RT by @sidhant: Panamá ha puesto su peso detrás de la oferta de la India para un escaño permanente en el Consejo de Seguridad de las Naciones Unidas, describiendo a Nueva Delhi como una voz vital para el Sur Global y un socio clave para defender el derecho internacional y los valores democráticos. En una entrevista exclusiva con Sidhant Sibal (@sidhant) de WION durante su visita a Delhi, el ministro de Relaciones Exteriores de Panamá, Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez (@javierachapma), enfatizó la necesidad de que las reformas de la ONU reflejen las realidades del siglo XXI "Hoy en día, la arquitectura global no refleja la realidad del siglo XXI", dijo
tweets.content:
Panamá ha puesto su peso detrás de la oferta de la India para un escaño permanente en el Consejo de Seguridad de las Naciones Unidas, describiendo a Nueva Delhi como una voz vital para el Sur Global y un socio clave para defender el derecho internacional y los valores democráticos.

En una entrevista exclusiva con Sidhant Sibal (@sidhant) de WION durante su visita a Delhi, el ministro de Relaciones Exteriores de Panamá, Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez (@javierachapma), enfatizó la necesidad de que las reformas de la ONU reflejen las realidades del siglo XXI

"Hoy en día, la arquitectura global no refleja la realidad del siglo XXI", dijo

WION (@WIONews)

Panama has thrown its weight behind India's bid for a permanent seat on the United Nations Security Council, describing New Delhi as a vital voice for the Global South and a key partner in upholding international law and democratic values.

In an exclusive interview with WION's Sidhant Sibal (@sidhant) during his visit to Delhi, Panama's Foreign Minister Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez (@javierachapma) emphasised the need for UN reforms to reflect 21st-century realities

"Today, the global architecture does not reflect the reality of the 21st century," he said

tweets.title: Most police forces in the country are overworked, underequipped, and outnumbered. Delhi Police is outnumbered when it comes to tackling a protest of 1 lakh+ people, which is now growing every day. The total strength of Delhi Police is around 83,000 personnel, responsible for policing 1,484 square kilometres. In peacetime, one Delhi Police personnel is outnumbered by approximately 259 civilians, let alone during a protest of this scale. A mob tends to develop a mentality to flank and attack when it sees security forces being outnumbered. To assert control when outnumbered, they have to use force. Additional reinforcement from the CRPF now seems necessary to control the situation, considering that everything is happening in a highly sensitive zone of the country.
tweets.content:
Most police forces in the country are overworked, underequipped, and outnumbered.

Delhi Police is outnumbered when it comes to tackling a protest of 1 lakh+ people, which is now growing every day. The total strength of Delhi Police is around 83,000 personnel, responsible for policing 1,484 square kilometres.

In peacetime, one Delhi Police personnel is outnumbered by approximately 259 civilians, let alone during a protest of this scale.

A mob tends to develop a mentality to flank and attack when it sees security forces being outnumbered.

To assert control when outnumbered, they have to use force.

Additional reinforcement from the CRPF now seems necessary to control the situation, considering that everything is happening in a highly sensitive zone of the country.

tweets.title: Apollo Micro Systems Limited (AMS) today announced that it has been awarded a Make-II Prototype Sanction Order (PSO) by the Indian Navy for the development of the SAVIOR-ASW the Semi- Submersible Autonomous Vessel for Intelligence, Operations and Reconnaissance. What is the SAVIOR-ASW System? SAVIOR-ASW is India's first indigenously developed semi-submersible autonomous vessel designed specifically for Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW) surveillance. It is an unmanned, self-powered maritime platform, yet capable of patrolling the ocean independently for longer period. It operates silently beneath the sea surface, using a sophisticated hydrophone array to listen for submarines and underwater threats, and transmits intelligence continuously to Naval command centres ashore and aboard ships entirely without human presence onboard.
tweets.content:
Apollo Micro Systems Limited (AMS) today announced that it has been awarded a Make-II Prototype
Sanction Order (PSO) by the Indian Navy for the development of the SAVIOR-ASW the Semi-
Submersible Autonomous Vessel for Intelligence, Operations and Reconnaissance.

What is the SAVIOR-ASW System?
SAVIOR-ASW is India's first indigenously developed semi-submersible autonomous vessel designed specifically for Anti-Submarine Warfare (ASW) surveillance.

It is an unmanned, self-powered
maritime platform, yet capable of patrolling the ocean independently for longer period. It operates silently beneath the sea surface, using a sophisticated hydrophone array to listen for submarines and
underwater threats, and transmits intelligence continuously to Naval command centres ashore and
aboard ships entirely without human presence onboard.

tweets.title: This will be the fourth time this government has failed to contain very manageable violence inside the country, that too in the national capital every single time. - The CAA/NRC protests turned violent without much ground. - Shaheen Bagh turned into the Delhi riots. - The farmers' protest turned into the most audacious dash up to the Red Fort. And now, this CJP protest. I keep seeing the same faces that I have seen before every violent protest. Nothing happens to anyone. This protest, violence, and anger among the youth will start costing the ruling party dearly in the coming times. But apart from the electoral impact, the sheer incompetence in managing very simple situations, maintaining positive optics, and keeping anarchist elements in check is astonishing. It is funny that managing these things is apparently so hard for the government that they end up beating students without effectively communicating to them what they are doing wrong. Good luck with this approach.
tweets.content:
This will be the fourth time this government has failed to contain very manageable violence inside the country, that too in the national capital every single time.

- The CAA/NRC protests turned violent without much ground.

- Shaheen Bagh turned into the Delhi riots.

- The farmers' protest turned into the most audacious dash up to the Red Fort.

And now, this CJP protest.

I keep seeing the same faces that I have seen before every violent protest. Nothing happens to anyone.

This protest, violence, and anger among the youth will start costing the ruling party dearly in the coming times.

But apart from the electoral impact, the sheer incompetence in managing very simple situations, maintaining positive optics, and keeping anarchist elements in check is astonishing.

It is funny that managing these things is apparently so hard for the government that they end up beating students without effectively communicating to them what they are doing wrong.

Good luck with this approach.

tweets.title: Interesting insight by Vice Admiral Raman Puri: DRDO wanted the EJ200 for the LCA, but a committee headed by an Air Marshal said no “the engine would be the GE F404”. With the EJ200, the aft fuselage would not have required significant changes. However, with the GE F404, the changes to the aft fuselage were substantial. The committee also recommended that the Kaveri engine program be closed.
tweets.content:
Interesting insight by Vice Admiral Raman Puri: DRDO wanted the EJ200 for the LCA, but a committee headed by an Air Marshal said no “the engine would be the GE F404”.

With the EJ200, the aft fuselage would not have required significant changes. However, with the GE F404, the changes to the aft fuselage were substantial. The committee also recommended that the Kaveri engine program be closed.
tweets.author: @alpha_defense

tweets.title: We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison. A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone. However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO). That’s the difference. In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset. This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.
tweets.content:
We often talk about defence and space together, so here’s an interesting comparison.

A few days ago, Vikram-1 made its first flight. If we draw a chronological analogy with defence, it is comparable to an LCA MK1 class milestone.

However, in defence, we have already invited a private-sector consortium to lead production of AMCA, a programme that, in terms of ambition and complexity, is far closer to SpaceX’s Starship (even though the design authority remains with DRDO).

That’s the difference.

In space, private industry is first proving itself on smaller launch vehicles before moving to heavy-lift systems. In defence, we’re asking private industry to participate in manufacturing one of our most ambitious combat aircraft programmes from the outset.

This is exactly what we had been saying for years: we haven’t fed our industry the basic tech and forcing them into AMCA. This is why space has revived early success in private efforts.

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: Congratulations, Pakistan. You're Finally No. 1. Not in education. Not in the economy. Not in innovation. According to the Global Terrorism Index 2026, Pakistan is now ranked as the world's most terrorism affected country, overtaking Burkina Faso. The report states that nearly 20% of all terrorism related deaths worldwide occurred in Pakistan. In other words, around one in every five people killed in terrorist attacks globally died in Pakistan. Source: https://www.visionofhumanity.org/wp-content/uploads/2026/03/Global-Terrorism-Index-2026-Report.pdf

Not in education.

The report states that nearly 20% of all terrorism related deaths worldwide occurred in Pakistan. In other words, around one in every five people killed in terrorist attacks globally died in Pakistan.

tweets.title: RT by @OsintTV: पाकिस्तान से ड्रोन के जरिए हथियार तस्करी के संगठित नेटवर्क का बड़ा खुलासा, बीकानेर पुलिस ने तीन और आरोपियों को किया गिरफ्तार, मुख्य हैंडलर से सीधे संपर्क और सीमा पार सिंडिकेट से जुड़े अहम सुराग मिले In a major breakthrough against cross-border arms smuggling, Bikaner Police have arrested three more accused linked to a Pakistan-based drone weapons trafficking network. Investigators revealed that the accused were in direct contact with the main handler and were responsible for retrieving drone-dropped consignments of illegal weapons using precise GPS coordinates before delivering them to designated recipients. The investigation has exposed an organised cross-border syndicate, and authorities are now expanding the probe to identify additional operatives and links across the India-Pakistan border. @BSF_India @BSF_Rajasthan @Bikaner_Police @IgpBikaner @FaridkotPolice @FaridkotRange @sgnrpolice @ips_mridul #BikanerPolice #RajasthanPolice #DroneSmuggling #Pakistan #BorderSecurity #IllegalArms #NationalSecurity #IndiaPakistanBorder #CrimeNews #BreakingNews #TINNetwork #DefenceJournalistSahil
tweets.content:
पाकिस्तान से ड्रोन के जरिए हथियार तस्करी के संगठित नेटवर्क का बड़ा खुलासा, बीकानेर पुलिस ने तीन और आरोपियों को किया गिरफ्तार, मुख्य हैंडलर से सीधे संपर्क और सीमा पार सिंडिकेट से जुड़े अहम सुराग मिले

In a major breakthrough against cross-border arms smuggling, Bikaner Police have arrested three more accused linked to a Pakistan-based drone weapons trafficking network. Investigators revealed that the accused were in direct contact with the main handler and were responsible for retrieving drone-dropped consignments of illegal weapons using precise GPS coordinates before delivering them to designated recipients. The investigation has exposed an organised cross-border syndicate, and authorities are now expanding the probe to identify additional operatives and links across the India-Pakistan border.
